methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Elementary Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Arye Barnehama that has raised $66M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of 9 companies in the same sector and stage cohort, consisting of 8 private firms and 1 public entity, with representatives including 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $66M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $112M. Peer-median valuation across the 9-company comp set produces $1.6B, with the peer universe ranging from $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within frontier robotics valuations at comparable stages.

The triangulated analysis produces a valuation range of $95M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median implied value of $856M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the tension between Elementary's capital raised and the elevated multiples observed in contemporaneous private robotics financings. Key uncertainties include Elementary's differentiation within the peer cohort, revenue scale relative to comparables, and whether the current peer median represents sustainable market pricing or froth in frontier-tech valuations.
